channel.title: Our Team on MIKE Research Clusters
channel.description: Recent content in Our Team on MIKE Research Clusters

item.title: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Suppawong Tuarob
item.description: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Suppawong Tuarob is an Associate Professor and the Head of the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering Research Clusters (MIKE). Recently recognized among the Top 100 Researchers, his work fundamentally targets the intersection of multi-disciplinary AI applications. Education Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering, The Pennsylvania State University M.S. in Science, Industrial Engineering, The Pennsylvania State University B.S. in Computer Science and Engineering, University of Michigan

item.title: Ar Kar
item.description: Biography I am a Master’s student in Computer Science (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) at Mahidol University, Faculty of ICT, and working on AI-driven educational technologies. My research focuses on learning analytics, student behavioral analysis, and AI-powered teaching assistants that enhance learning outcomes.

item.title: Pat Vatiwutipong
item.description: Biography I am a PhD student working on explainable machine learning and healthcare data modeling. My research focuses on developing interpretable representations of healthcare data, particularly through methods that balance predictive performance with clinical explanation. I am interested in collaborating with researchers in machine learning, medical informatics, and mathematical modeling.

item.title: Phaphontee Yamchote
item.description: Biography I am a PhD student in Computer Science with research interests in mathematical logic, computational logic in AI, algorithmic machine learning, and data science. My current work focuses on learning representations of incomplete tabular data using graph neural networks. I am interested in research that combines mathematical rigor with practical machine learning applications, especially in problems involving structured data and feature interactions. I am open to collaboration with researchers and professionals in AI, machine learning, and related fields.

item.title: Pisut Manorat
item.description: Biography I am a PhD student working on causality in continuous-time event streams. My research focuses on investigating the assumptions for discovering causal structure from event streams and how to integrate the causal knowledge into machine learning models, allowing them to perform causal reasoning tasks. My research aims to facilitate the development of robust machine learning systems that can be used to support decision-making process in high-stake domains where experiments can be difficult to perform, e.

item.title: Prahyat Puangjaktha
item.description: Biography I am a PhD Student passionate about using data to understand environmental challenges. My work focuses on the intersection of Environmental Science and Data Science. Currently, I am studying to apply AI and Remote Sensing for Environmental Challenges and Disasters. I am driven to produce research that translates into actionable insights for environmental management in Thailand. I am always open to connecting with fellow researchers, data scientists, and environmental professionals.
